prompt: “Il veut” vs “ils veulent” — in speech you hear the difference because…
options: veut ends in a vowel sound, veulent ends in an L sound
explain: il veut /vø/ vs ils veulent /vœl/ — the verb itself tells you it's plural.

prompt: “Il n'y a pas de pain.” — after “pas”, “un/une/du” usually becomes…
explain: Negation squeezes articles to “de”: il y a du pain → il n'y a pas DE pain.

prompt: “ils ont” vs “ils sont” — how do you tell them apart by ear?
options: ils‿ont has a Z liaison (they have); ils sont has an S sound (they are)
they sound identical, only context helps
ils sont has a longer O
explain: /il.zɔ̃/ = they have; /il.sɔ̃/ = they are. This Z-vs-S pair is a classic exam trap.

options: they — a group of women/feminine nouns
explain: One man in the group → ils.
il y a in the negative.
